alternate_names: 国家互联网信息办公室
notes: China's primary internet and cyberspace regulator. Responsible for AI regulation, data security, content moderation, and cybersecurity oversight. Part of the Central Cyberspace Affairs Commission.

match_nodes.description: Interim Measures for the Management of Generative Artificial Intelligence Services

data.official_name: Interim Measures for the Management of Generative Artificial Intelligence Services
alternate_names: 生成式人工智能服务管理暂行办法
data.description: Regulates generative AI services in China. Requires security assessments, content moderation, and data compliance.
data.notes: Effective August 15, 2023. Jointly issued by CAC, NDRC, Ministry of Education, MOST, MIIT, MPS, and SARFT. Requires providers to: conduct security assessments before public launch, implement content moderation, ensure training data legality, protect user privacy, and prevent generation of content that subverts state power or promotes terrorism. Applies to services offered to the public within China.

data.official_name: Personal Information Protection Law of the People's Republic of China
alternate_names: 中华人民共和国个人信息保护法
data.description: China's comprehensive personal information protection law (effective November 2021). Imposes GDPR-like obligations including consent, data minimisation, cross-border transfer restrictions, and DPO requirements.
data.notes: Companion to the Cybersecurity Law (CSL 2017) and Data Security Law (DSL 2021) — together form China's data-governance trinity.

data.official_name: Data Security Law of the People's Republic of China
alternate_names: 中华人民共和国数据安全法
data.description: China's data security law establishing tiered data classification (important data, core data), security obligations across the data lifecycle, and cross-border transfer restrictions for industrial / non-personal data. Effective September 2021.
data.notes: Operates alongside PIPL (personal information) and CSL (cybersecurity).

data.official_name: Cybersecurity Law of the People's Republic of China
alternate_names: 中华人民共和国网络安全法
data.description: China's foundational cybersecurity law (effective June 2017). Establishes the Multi-Level Protection Scheme (MLPS, operationalised via GB/T 22239), critical information infrastructure (CII) designation, network operator obligations, and cross-border data transfer constraints.
data.notes: Foundation for the broader Chinese cyber-governance framework.
